content: {"recommendations": [{"section_id": "s01", "section_title": "Overview and Definitions of Intermittent Fasting and Metabolic Syndrome", "diagram_type": "comparison_matrix", "description": "A 5-protocol comparison matrix (TRE 16:8, TRE 18:6, ADF, 5:2, FMD) with rows for: cycle length, eating window, caloric restriction depth, behavioral complexity, and clinical research density. This visualizes the protocol family clearly and lets readers quickly identify the appropriate protocol for a given patient context."}, {"section_id": "s02", "section_title": "Pooled Effect Sizes on Metabolic Outcomes", "diagram_type": "ranking", "description": "A ranked bar chart of pooled effect sizes by endpoint: FBS (SMD = -0.51), HOMA-IR (SMD = -0.39), HbA1c (SMD = -0.25), insulin (SMD = -0.27), body weight (-3.59 kg), SBP (-5.54 mmHg), and LDL-C (-56.22 mg/dL), each with 95% CI error bars. Standardizes the comparison across endpoints with different units."}, {"section_id": "s04", "section_title": "Long-term Cardiovascular Safety and the AHA 2024 Signal", "diagram_type": "comparison_matrix", "description": "A side-by-side comparison of the AHA 2024 NHANES analysis vs the Ramadan systematic-review evidence: study type (observational vs observational), population (~20,078 NHANES vs hundreds of millions of Muslim adults), exposure measurement (2-day dietary recall vs religiously-defined daily fast), follow-up duration (8y median vs annual fasting cycles), and key finding (91% higher CVD mortality with <8h windows vs no CVD elevation during Ramadan). Visualizes the methodological tension."}, {"section_id": "s06", "section_title": "Mechanisms — Gut Microbiome, Circadian, and Autophagy Pathways", "diagram_type": "process_flow", "description": "A pathway diagram from intermittent fasting → metabolic switch (glucose → ketones) → ILC3/IL-22 axis → adipose tissue beigeing AND circadian alignment → mTOR/AMPK signaling → autophagy → improved insulin sensitivity. Multiple parallel mechanism axes converging on cardiometabolic improvement."}, {"section_id": "s09", "section_title": "Clinical Implications, Patient Selection, and Knowledge Gaps", "diagram_type": "decision_tree", "description": "A clinical decision tree for patient selection: BMI ≥30? motivated? eating disorder history? on insulin/sulfonylureas? established CVD? → branches into (a) Suitable: 16:8 TRE first-line, (b) Marginal: counseled with caveats, (c) Excluded: alternative interventions. Operationalizes the patient-selection framework in the section."}]}
